# i18n

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `i18n`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `i18n.glibcLocales` | `absolute path` | Customized `glibcLocales` package providing the `LOCALE_ARCHIVE_*` environment variable. This option only applies to the Linux platform. If Home Manager is installed as a NixOS submodule, this option defaults to NixOS' {var}`i18n.glibcLocales`. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable an additional input method type. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enabled` | `null or one of "fcitx", "fcitx5", "nabi", "uim", "hime", "kime"` | Deprecated - use `type` and `enable = true` instead |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.addons` | `list of package` | Enabled Fcitx5 addons. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.fcitx5-with-addons` | `package` | The fcitx5 package to use. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.ignoreUserConfig` | `boolean` | Ignore the user configures. **Warning**: When this is enabled, the user config files are totally ignored and the user dict can't be saved and loaded. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.quickPhrase` | `attribute set of string` | Quick phrases. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.quickPhraseFiles` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Quick phrase files. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.addons` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | The addon configures in `conf` folder in ini format with global sections. Each item is written to the corresponding file. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.addons.<name>.globalSection` | `section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | global section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string)) |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.addons.<name>.sections` | `attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string)) |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.globalOptions` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | The global options in `config` file in ini format. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.inputMethod` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | The input method configure in `profile` file in ini format. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes` | `lazy attribute set of (submodule)` | Themes to be written to {file}`$XDG_DATA_HOME/fcitx5/themes/${name}` |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes.<name>.highlightImage` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the SVG of the highlight. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes.<name>.panelImage` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the SVG of the panel. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes.<name>.theme` | `null or (attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))) or strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path` | The `theme.conf` file of the theme. See https://fcitx-im.org/wiki/Fcitx_5_Theme#Background_images for more information. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.waylandFrontend` | `boolean` | Use the Wayland input method frontend. See [Using Fcitx 5 on Wayland](https://fcitx-im.org/wiki/Using_Fcitx_5_on_Wayland). |
| `i18n.inputMethod.kime.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | kime configuration. Refer to <https://github.com/Riey/kime/blob/develop/docs/CONFIGURATION.md> for details on supported values. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.type` | `null or one of "fcitx", "fcitx5", "nabi", "uim", "hime", "kime"` | Select the enabled input method. Input methods are software to input symbols that are not available on standard input devices. Input methods are especially used to input Chinese, Japanese and Korean characters. Currently the following input methods are available in Home Manager: `fcitx5` : A customizable lightweight input method. The next generation of fcitx. Addons (including engines, dictionaries, skins) can be added using [](#opt-i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.addons). `nabi` : A Korean input method based on XIM. Nabi doesn't support Qt 5. `uim` : The "universal input method" is a library with an XIM bridge. uim mainly supports Chinese, Japanese and Korean. `hime` : An extremely easy-to-use input method framework. `kime` : A Korean IME. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.uim.toolbar` | `one of "gtk", "gtk3", "gtk-systray", "gtk3-systray", "qt4"` | Selected UIM toolbar. |
